PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient: Margaret Moore
MRN: patient_5915b7eaad78
Specimen ID: S2023-79105
Date of Procedure: 2023-09-24
Date of Report: 2023-09-27

SPECIMEN: Right upper lobe of lung, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
Adenocarcinoma, poorly differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 6.7 cm
Margins: Negative (closest margin 0.3 cm)
Lymphovascular invasion: Present
Perineural invasion: Not identified

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
TTF-1: Positive
Napsin-A: Positive
CK7: Positive
CK20: Negative
PD-L1 (22C3): TPS 40%

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Margaret Moore - Right upper lobe of lung" is a tan-white tissue fragment measuring 6.7 x 4.7 x 3.4 cm. The cut surface reveals a friable gray-white mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A5.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a poorly differentiated neoplasm composed of cords and trabeculae of atypical cells with moderate cytoplasm and vesicular chromatin. Mitotic figures are frequent (12 per 10 HPF). No necrosis is identified. The surrounding stroma shows minimal reactive changes.

Electronically signed by: James Park, MD
Department of Pathology

PET/CT REPORT

Patient: Margaret Moore
MRN: patient_5915b7eaad78
Date of Study: 2023-09-24

CLINICAL INDICATION: Adenocarcinoma of the right upper lobe of lung. baseline staging.

COMPARISON: None available.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT performed from skull base to iliac crest following administration of 100 mL Omnipaque 350 IV contrast.

FINDINGS:
LUNGS: Spiculated mass in the right upper lobe of lung measuring 4.6 x 3.7 cm. New finding.

MEDIASTINUM: Paratracheal lymphadenopathy, largest node measuring 1.8 cm in short axis.

LIVER: Homogeneous attenuation. No focal lesions.

PANCREAS: Normal in size and attenuation. No pancreatic mass or ductal dilation.

KIDNEYS: Normal in size bilaterally. No hydronephrosis or renal masses.

PELVIS: No pelvic mass or lymphadenopathy. Bladder unremarkable.

BONES: No suspicious osseous lesions. Degenerative changes of the lumbar spine.

IMPRESSION:
1. Right upper lobe mass consistent with primary lung malignancy, as described.
2. Mediastinal lymphadenopathy, likely nodal metastatic disease.

Electronically signed by: Robert Chang, MD
Department of Radiology

INITIAL CONSULTATION - Thoracic Surgery

Patient: Margaret Moore
MRN: patient_5915b7eaad78
Date: 2023-10-02
Provider: Dr. Richard Okafor

CHIEF COMPLAINT: initial oncology consultation

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Mr./Ms. Moore is a 69-year-old female who presents for initial evaluation of newly diagnosed adenocarcinoma of the right upper lobe of lung, IIIA. Patient presented with symptoms prompting workup. Stage III non-small cell lung cancer with EGFR L858R mutation. Pathology confirmed adenocarcinoma. Molecular testing is pending. Patient reports fatigue and unintentional 10-lb weight loss over 3 months.

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY:
- peripheral vascular disease
- chronic kidney disease stage 2
- osteoarthritis

SURGICAL HISTORY:
- Cholecystectomy (age 50)

SOCIAL HISTORY:
- Current smoker (1 ppd)
- Alcohol: Social drinker (2-3 drinks/week)
- Occupation: Construction worker (retired)
- Lives with spouse and adult children

FAMILY HISTORY:
- Father with prostate cancer at age 68. Paternal uncle with lung cancer.

ALLERGIES: Iodine contrast (anaphylaxis)

MEDICATIONS:
- None

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
Positive for: night sweats, intermittent nausea, unintentional weight loss, abdominal bloating. Remainder of 14-point ROS reviewed and negative.

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
Vitals: BP 142/66, HR 90, RR 15, Temp 97.7F, SpO2 97% on RA, Wt 80.1 kg
General: Comfortable, thin-appearing, no acute distress.
HEENT: Normocephalic, atraumatic. Mucous membranes moist.
Neck: No thyromegaly. No lymphadenopathy.
Lungs: Clear bilaterally. No rales or rhonchi.
CV: RRR, S1/S2 normal. No murmurs, gallops, or rubs.
Abdomen: Soft, mildly tender in RUQ. No rebound. Normoactive bowel sounds.
Extremities: Trace bilateral lower extremity edema. Warm and well-perfused.
Neuro: A&O x3, CN II-XII intact, no focal deficits.

LABORATORY RESULTS:
WBC 4.0, Hgb 9.7, Plt 242, Cr 1.03

IMAGING:
See separate imaging report.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
69-year-old female with IIIA adenocarcinoma of the right upper lobe of lung. initial oncology consultation.

1. Adenocarcinoma of the right upper lobe of lung, Stage IIIA
   - Reviewed pathology and imaging with patient and family
   - Recommend neoadjuvant chemotherapy
   - Will discuss at multidisciplinary tumor board
   - Molecular profiling results pending
2. Supportive care
   - Nutrition consultation placed
   - Social work referral for psychosocial support
   - Peripheral access adequate
3. Follow-up in 1-2 weeks to initiate treatment

Electronically signed by: Amanda Foster, MD
Thoracic Surgery

NEXT GENERATION SEQUENCING REPORT

Patient: Margaret Moore
MRN: patient_5915b7eaad78
Date of Report: 2023-10-11
Specimen: Right upper lobe of lung (Resection)
Diagnosis: Adenocarcinoma
Panel: FoundationOne CDx (324 genes)

RESULTS SUMMARY:
Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB): 3.2 mutations/Mb (Low)
Microsatellite Status: MSS

CLINICALLY SIGNIFICANT GENOMIC ALTERATIONS:
  EGFR p.L858R (c.2573T>G) - Missense (Exon 21) [Clinically Significant]

ADDITIONAL PATHOGENIC ALTERATIONS:
  TP53 p.R248W - Missense (Exon 7)
  RB1 Loss - Deletion

VARIANTS OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE:
  ATM p.V2424G - VUS
  BRCA2 p.K1003E - VUS

COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS:
  No significant copy number alterations detected

FUSIONS:
  No ALK, ROS1, RET, or NTRK fusions detected.

THERAPY ASSOCIATIONS:
  - EGFR L858R: FDA-approved targeted therapies include osimertinib (preferred), erlotinib, afatinib, gefitinib.
  - PD-L1 expression should be assessed for immunotherapy eligibility.

Note: This test was performed using hybrid capture-based next generation sequencing.
Sequencing depth: mean 686x coverage.
Tumor content: 29%.

Electronically signed by: Maria Santos, MD, PhD
Molecular Pathology Laboratory

SPECIMEN: Right upper lobe of lung, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
Adenocarcinoma, moderately differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 5.1 cm
Margins: Negative (closest margin 0.5 cm)
Lymphovascular invasion: Not identified
Perineural invasion: Present

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
TTF-1: Positive
Napsin-A: Positive
CK7: Positive
CK20: Negative
PD-L1 (22C3): TPS 40%

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Margaret Moore - Right upper lobe of lung" is a tan-white tissue fragment measuring 5.1 x 3.6 x 2.5 cm. The cut surface reveals a soft white mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A9.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a moderately differentiated neoplasm composed of glands of atypical cells with abundant cytoplasm and vesicular chromatin. Mitotic figures are occasionally seen (3 per 10 HPF). Necrosis is present. The surrounding stroma shows desmoplastic reaction.

NEXT GENERATION SEQUENCING REPORT

Patient: Patricia White
MRN: patient_e28bd3f7bece
Date of Report: 2022-02-08
Specimen: Right upper lobe of lung (FNA cell block)
Diagnosis: Adenocarcinoma
Panel: Guardant360 CDx (523 genes)

RESULTS SUMMARY:
Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB): 6.2 mutations/Mb (Low)
Microsatellite Status: MSS

CLINICALLY SIGNIFICANT GENOMIC ALTERATIONS:
  EGFR p.L858R (c.2573T>G) - Missense (Exon 21) [Clinically Significant]

ADDITIONAL PATHOGENIC ALTERATIONS:
  TP53 p.R248W - Missense (Exon 7)
  RB1 Loss - Deletion

VARIANTS OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE:
  ATM p.V2424G - VUS
  BRCA2 p.K1003E - VUS

COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS:
  No significant copy number alterations detected

FUSIONS:
  No ALK, ROS1, RET, or NTRK fusions detected.

THERAPY ASSOCIATIONS:
  - EGFR L858R: FDA-approved targeted therapies include osimertinib (preferred), erlotinib, afatinib, gefitinib.
  - PD-L1 expression should be assessed for immunotherapy eligibility.

Note: This test was performed using hybrid capture-based next generation sequencing.
Sequencing depth: mean 450x coverage.
Tumor content: 71%.

NEXT GENERATION SEQUENCING REPORT

Patient: Thomas Clark
MRN: patient_e56d6f8b8515
Date of Report: 2022-11-07
Specimen: Right upper lobe of lung (Biopsy)
Diagnosis: Adenocarcinoma
Panel: MSK-IMPACT (523 genes)

RESULTS SUMMARY:
Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB): 6.1 mutations/Mb (Low)
Microsatellite Status: MSS

CLINICALLY SIGNIFICANT GENOMIC ALTERATIONS:
  EGFR p.L858R (c.2573T>G) - Missense (Exon 21) [Clinically Significant]

ADDITIONAL PATHOGENIC ALTERATIONS:
  RB1 Loss - Deletion

VARIANTS OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE:
  ATM p.V2424G - VUS
  BRCA2 p.K1003E - VUS

COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS:
  No significant copy number alterations detected

FUSIONS:
  No ALK, ROS1, RET, or NTRK fusions detected.

THERAPY ASSOCIATIONS:
  - EGFR L858R: FDA-approved targeted therapies include osimertinib (preferred), erlotinib, afatinib, gefitinib.
  - PD-L1 expression should be assessed for immunotherapy eligibility.

Note: This test was performed using hybrid capture-based next generation sequencing.
Sequencing depth: mean 493x coverage.
Tumor content: 42%.

CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH CONTRAST REPORT

Patient: Thomas Clark
MRN: patient_e56d6f8b8515
Date of Study: 2023-09-28

CLINICAL INDICATION: Adenocarcinoma of the right upper lobe of lung. surveillance imaging.

COMPARISON: Prior study dated approximately 6 weeks ago available for comparison.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT performed from skull base to iliac crest following administration of 100 mL Omnipaque 350 IV contrast.

FINDINGS:
LUNGS: Irregular mass in the right upper lobe of lung measuring 1.7 x 1.4 cm. Decreased in size compared to prior.

MEDIASTINUM: Paratracheal lymphadenopathy, largest node measuring 1.5 cm in short axis.

LIVER: Homogeneous attenuation. No focal lesions.

PANCREAS: Normal in size and attenuation. No pancreatic mass or ductal dilation.

KIDNEYS: Normal in size bilaterally. No hydronephrosis or renal masses.

PELVIS: No pelvic mass or lymphadenopathy. Bladder unremarkable.

BONES: No suspicious osseous lesions. Degenerative changes of the lumbar spine.

IMPRESSION:
1. Right upper lobe mass consistent with primary lung malignancy, decreased.
2. Mediastinal lymphadenopathy, likely nodal metastatic disease.
3. Overall assessment: Partial response per RECIST 1.1.

Patient: Ashley Perez

Specimen ID: S2023-23853
Date of Procedure: 2023-07-17
Date of Report: 2023-07-22

SPECIMEN: Right upper lobe of lung, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
Adenocarcinoma, poorly differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 2.2 cm
Margins: Negative (closest margin 0.5 cm)
Lymphovascular invasion: Present
Perineural invasion: Not identified

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
TTF-1: Positive
Napsin-A: Positive
CK7: Positive
CK20: Negative
PD-L1 (22C3): TPS 40%

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Ashley Perez - Right upper lobe of lung" is a tan-white tissue fragment measuring 2.2 x 1.5 x 1.1 cm. The cut surface reveals a firm gray-white mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A6.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a poorly differentiated neoplasm composed of glands of atypical cells with scant cytoplasm and high nuclear-to-cytoplasmic ratio. Mitotic figures are occasionally seen (3 per 10 HPF). No necrosis is identified. The surrounding stroma shows fibrosis.

NEXT GENERATION SEQUENCING REPORT

Patient: Ashley Perez
MRN: patient_f24ddd4938cc
Date of Report: 2023-08-01
Specimen: Right upper lobe of lung (FNA cell block)
Diagnosis: Adenocarcinoma
Panel: Guardant360 CDx (468 genes)

RESULTS SUMMARY:
Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB): 7.7 mutations/Mb (Low)
Microsatellite Status: MSS

CLINICALLY SIGNIFICANT GENOMIC ALTERATIONS:
  EGFR p.L858R (c.2573T>G) - Missense (Exon 21) [Clinically Significant]

ADDITIONAL PATHOGENIC ALTERATIONS:
  RB1 Loss - Deletion

VARIANTS OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE:
  ATM p.V2424G - VUS
  BRCA2 p.K1003E - VUS

COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS:
  No significant copy number alterations detected

FUSIONS:
  No ALK, ROS1, RET, or NTRK fusions detected.

THERAPY ASSOCIATIONS:
  - EGFR L858R: FDA-approved targeted therapies include osimertinib (preferred), erlotinib, afatinib, gefitinib.
  - PD-L1 expression should be assessed for immunotherapy eligibility.

Note: This test was performed using hybrid capture-based next generation sequencing.
Sequencing depth: mean 418x coverage.
Tumor content: 50%.

Patient: Jennifer Perez
MRN: patient_2812c8f3ff93
Date of Report: 2022-08-10
Specimen: Right upper lobe of lung (FNA cell block)
Diagnosis: Adenocarcinoma
Panel: Guardant360 CDx (648 genes)

RESULTS SUMMARY:

Microsatellite Status: MSS

VARIANTS OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE:
  ATM p.V2424G - VUS

COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS:
  No significant copy number alterations detected

FUSIONS:
  No ALK, ROS1, RET, or NTRK fusions detected.

THERAPY ASSOCIATIONS:
  - EGFR L858R: FDA-approved targeted therapies include osimertinib (preferred), erlotinib, afatinib, gefitinib.
  - PD-L1 expression should be assessed for immunotherapy eligibility.

Note: This test was performed using hybrid capture-based next generation sequencing.
Sequencing depth: mean 659x coverage.
Tumor content: 41%.

Patient: Cynthia Jones

Specimen ID: S2023-16402
Date of Procedure: 2023-01-08
Date of Report: 2023-01-11

SPECIMEN: Left breast, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
Invasive ductal carcinoma, poorly differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 4.2 cm
Margins: Negative (closest margin 1.2 cm)
Lymphovascular invasion: Not identified
Perineural invasion: Not identified

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
ER: Negative (0%)
PR: Negative (0%)
HER2 IHC: 3+ (positive)
Ki-67: 45%

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Cynthia Jones - Left breast" is a gray-white tissue fragment measuring 4.2 x 2.9 x 2.1 cm. The cut surface reveals a friable tan mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A7.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a poorly differentiated neoplasm composed of sheets of atypical cells with abundant cytoplasm and prominent nucleoli. Mitotic figures are occasionally seen (3 per 10 HPF). Focal necrosis is present. The surrounding stroma shows minimal reactive changes.

Patient: Cynthia Jones

Date of Report: 2023-01-19

Diagnosis: Invasive ductal carcinoma

  ERBB2 Amplification (copy number 12) - Amplification [Clinically Significant]

  PIK3CA p.H1047R - Missense (Exon 20)

VARIANTS OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE:
  CHEK2 p.I157T - VUS
  PALB2 p.L939W - VUS

COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS:
  No significant copy number alterations detected

FUSIONS:
  No pathogenic fusions detected.

THERAPY ASSOCIATIONS:
  - ERBB2 amplification: FDA-approved HER2-directed therapies include trastuzumab, pertuzumab, T-DXd, tucatinib.
  - PIK3CA H1047R: Alpelisib may be considered in HR+/HER2- setting.

Note: This test was performed using hybrid capture-based next generation sequencing.
Sequencing depth: mean 567x coverage.
Tumor content: 38%.

Sections show a moderately differentiated neoplasm composed of glands of atypical cells with scant cytoplasm and irregular nuclear contours. Mitotic figures are identified (6 per 10 HPF). No necrosis is identified. The surrounding stroma shows chronic inflammation.

Margins: Positive at inked margin
Lymphovascular invasion: Present
Perineural invasion: Not identified

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
ER: Negative (0%)

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a well differentiated neoplasm composed of sheets of atypical cells with moderate cytoplasm and high nuclear-to-cytoplasmic ratio. Mitotic figures are frequent (12 per 10 HPF). Necrosis is present. The surrounding stroma shows chronic inflammation.

NEXT GENERATION SEQUENCING REPORT

Patient: Jeffrey Wilson
MRN: patient_9bb40ad67699
Date of Report: 2022-11-19
Specimen: Sigmoid colon (Resection)
Diagnosis: Moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma
Panel: FoundationOne CDx (648 genes)

RESULTS SUMMARY:
Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB): 5.6 mutations/Mb (Low)
Microsatellite Status: MSS

CLINICALLY SIGNIFICANT GENOMIC ALTERATIONS:
  KRAS Wild-type - No mutation [Clinically Significant]

ADDITIONAL PATHOGENIC ALTERATIONS:
  TP53 p.R175H - Missense (Exon 5)
  APC p.R1450* (c.4348C>T) - Nonsense (Exon 16)

VARIANTS OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE:
  SMAD4 p.R361C - VUS
  FBXW7 p.R465H - VUS

COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS:
  No significant copy number alterations detected

FUSIONS:
  No pathogenic fusions detected.

THERAPY ASSOCIATIONS:
  - KRAS wild-type: Patient eligible for anti-EGFR therapy (cetuximab, panitumumab) if disease progresses.
  - MSS: Unlikely to benefit from single-agent immune checkpoint inhibitor.

Note: This test was performed using hybrid capture-based next generation sequencing.
Sequencing depth: mean 796x coverage.
Tumor content: 52%.

NEXT GENERATION SEQUENCING REPORT

Patient: Jeffrey Hill
MRN: patient_70096e77249d
Date of Report: 2023-09-12
Specimen: Sigmoid colon (FNA cell block)
Diagnosis: Moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma
Panel: MSK-IMPACT (648 genes)

RESULTS SUMMARY:
Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB): 3.5 mutations/Mb (Low)
Microsatellite Status: MSS

CLINICALLY SIGNIFICANT GENOMIC ALTERATIONS:
  KRAS Wild-type - No mutation [Clinically Significant]

ADDITIONAL PATHOGENIC ALTERATIONS:
  APC p.R1450* (c.4348C>T) - Nonsense (Exon 16)
  TP53 p.R175H - Missense (Exon 5)

VARIANTS OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE:
  SMAD4 p.R361C - VUS
  FBXW7 p.R465H - VUS

COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS:
  No significant copy number alterations detected

FUSIONS:
  No pathogenic fusions detected.

THERAPY ASSOCIATIONS:
  - KRAS wild-type: Patient eligible for anti-EGFR therapy (cetuximab, panitumumab) if disease progresses.
  - MSS: Unlikely to benefit from single-agent immune checkpoint inhibitor.

Note: This test was performed using hybrid capture-based next generation sequencing.
Sequencing depth: mean 753x coverage.
Tumor content: 64%.

Diagnosis: Moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma

COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS:
  No significant copy number alterations detected

FUSIONS:
  No pathogenic fusions detected.

THERAPY ASSOCIATIONS:
  - KRAS wild-type: Patient eligible for anti-EGFR therapy (cetuximab, panitumumab) if disease progresses.
  - MSS: Unlikely to benefit from single-agent immune checkpoint inhibitor.

Sequencing depth: mean 796x coverage.
Tumor content: 55%.

  CDKN2A homozygous deletion

FUSIONS:

THERAPY ASSOCIATIONS:
  - BRAF V600E: FDA-approved BRAF/MEK inhibitors include dabrafenib/trametinib, encorafenib/binimetinib, vemurafenib/cobimetinib.

Sequencing depth: mean 519x coverage.
Tumor content: 37%.

Diagnosis: Malignant melanoma

  CDKN2A Loss - Homozygous deletion

VARIANTS OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE:
  NF1 p.T1556M - VUS
  ARID2 p.Q1334L - VUS

COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS:
  CDKN2A homozygous deletion

FUSIONS:
  No pathogenic fusions detected.

THERAPY ASSOCIATIONS:
  - BRAF V600E: FDA-approved BRAF/MEK inhibitors include dabrafenib/trametinib, encorafenib/binimetinib, vemurafenib/cobimetinib.

Note: This test was performed using hybrid capture-based next generation sequencing.
Sequencing depth: mean 736x coverage.
Tumor content: 44%.

  CDKN2A homozygous deletion

NEXT GENERATION SEQUENCING REPORT

Patient: Melissa Scott
MRN: patient_cf85d5603332
Date of Report: 2022-03-31
Specimen: Right ovary (Biopsy)
Diagnosis: High-grade serous carcinoma
Panel: MSK-IMPACT (324 genes)

RESULTS SUMMARY:
Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB): 8.0 mutations/Mb (Low)
Microsatellite Status: MSS

CLINICALLY SIGNIFICANT GENOMIC ALTERATIONS:
  BRCA1 p.E1836fs (c.5503delA) - Frameshift (Exon 22) [Clinically Significant]

ADDITIONAL PATHOGENIC ALTERATIONS:
  NF1 p.R1947* - Nonsense (Exon 39)
  TP53 p.C176Y - Missense (Exon 5)

VARIANTS OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE:
  CDK12 p.T826A - VUS
  ARID1A p.D1850N - VUS

COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS:
  No significant copy number alterations detected

FUSIONS:
  No pathogenic fusions detected.

THERAPY ASSOCIATIONS:
  - BRCA1 pathogenic variant: FDA-approved PARP inhibitors include olaparib, niraparib, rucaparib.
  - Platinum sensitivity expected.

Note: This test was performed using hybrid capture-based next generation sequencing.
Sequencing depth: mean 794x coverage.
Tumor content: 63%.

NEXT GENERATION SEQUENCING REPORT

Patient: Margaret Martin
MRN: patient_150ab7a3f646
Date of Report: 2023-06-09
Specimen: Right ovary (Core needle biopsy)
Diagnosis: High-grade serous carcinoma
Panel: Guardant360 CDx (648 genes)

RESULTS SUMMARY:
Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB): 3.6 mutations/Mb (Low)
Microsatellite Status: MSS

CLINICALLY SIGNIFICANT GENOMIC ALTERATIONS:
  BRCA1 p.E1836fs (c.5503delA) - Frameshift (Exon 22) [Clinically Significant]

ADDITIONAL PATHOGENIC ALTERATIONS:
  TP53 p.C176Y - Missense (Exon 5)
  NF1 p.R1947* - Nonsense (Exon 39)

VARIANTS OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE:
  CDK12 p.T826A - VUS
  ARID1A p.D1850N - VUS

COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS:
  No significant copy number alterations detected

FUSIONS:
  No pathogenic fusions detected.

THERAPY ASSOCIATIONS:
  - BRCA1 pathogenic variant: FDA-approved PARP inhibitors include olaparib, niraparib, rucaparib.
  - Platinum sensitivity expected.

Note: This test was performed using hybrid capture-based next generation sequencing.
Sequencing depth: mean 669x coverage.
Tumor content: 47%.

Molecular Pathology Laboratory

Specimen: Right ovary (Resection)
Diagnosis: High-grade serous carcinoma
Panel: FoundationOne CDx (324 genes)

RESULTS SUMMARY:

Microsatellite Status: MSS

CLINICALLY SIGNIFICANT GENOMIC ALTERATIONS:
  BRCA1 p.E1836fs (c.5503delA) - Frameshift (Exon 22) [Clinically Significant]

ADDITIONAL PATHOGENIC ALTERATIONS:
  NF1 p.R1947* - Nonsense (Exon 39)
  TP53 p.C176Y - Missense (Exon 5)

VARIANTS OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE:
  CDK12 p.T826A - VUS

COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS:
  No significant copy number alterations detected

FUSIONS:
  No pathogenic fusions detected.

THERAPY ASSOCIATIONS:
  - BRCA1 pathogenic variant: FDA-approved PARP inhibitors include olaparib, niraparib, rucaparib.
  - Platinum sensitivity expected.

Note: This test was performed using hybrid capture-based next generation sequencing.
Sequencing depth: mean 427x coverage.
Tumor content: 46%.

PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient: Mary Davis
MRN: patient_4fe95d2bca83
Specimen ID: S2023-50984
Date of Procedure: 2023-06-16
Date of Report: 2023-06-20

SPECIMEN: Right ovary, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
High-grade serous carcinoma, poorly differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 6.5 cm
Margins: Negative (closest margin 0.5 cm)
Lymphovascular invasion: Present
Perineural invasion: Not identified

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
PAX8: Positive
WT1: Positive
P53: Aberrant (overexpression)
CK7: Positive
CK20: Negative

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Mary Davis - Right ovary" is a gray-white tissue fragment measuring 6.5 x 4.5 x 3.2 cm. The cut surface reveals a friable gray-white mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A8.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a poorly differentiated neoplasm composed of nests of atypical cells with moderate cytoplasm and irregular nuclear contours. Mitotic figures are occasionally seen (3 per 10 HPF). No necrosis is identified. The surrounding stroma shows chronic inflammation.

Patient: Amanda Johnson

Date of Report: 2022-10-23
Specimen: Right ovary (Biopsy)
Diagnosis: High-grade serous carcinoma
Panel: Tempus xT (468 genes)

RESULTS SUMMARY:
Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB): 7.5 mutations/Mb (Low)
Microsatellite Status: MSS

CLINICALLY SIGNIFICANT GENOMIC ALTERATIONS:
  BRCA1 p.E1836fs (c.5503delA) - Frameshift (Exon 22) [Clinically Significant]

ADDITIONAL PATHOGENIC ALTERATIONS:
  TP53 p.C176Y - Missense (Exon 5)

VARIANTS OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE:
  CDK12 p.T826A - VUS
  ARID1A p.D1850N - VUS

COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS:
  No significant copy number alterations detected

FUSIONS:
  No pathogenic fusions detected.

THERAPY ASSOCIATIONS:
  - BRCA1 pathogenic variant: FDA-approved PARP inhibitors include olaparib, niraparib, rucaparib.
  - Platinum sensitivity expected.

Note: This test was performed using hybrid capture-based next generation sequencing.
Sequencing depth: mean 493x coverage.
Tumor content: 23%.

Sections show a moderately differentiated neoplasm composed of glands of atypical cells with moderate cytoplasm and prominent nucleoli. Mitotic figures are identified (6 per 10 HPF). No necrosis is identified. The surrounding stroma shows chronic inflammation.

  ATM p.S1893R - VUS

COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS:
  No significant copy number alterations detected

FUSIONS:
  No pathogenic fusions detected.

THERAPY ASSOCIATIONS:
  - BRCA2 pathogenic variant: FDA-approved PARP inhibitor olaparib indicated for HRR-mutated mCRPC.
  - Consider platinum-based chemotherapy.

Note: This test was performed using hybrid capture-based next generation sequencing.
Sequencing depth: mean 648x coverage.
Tumor content: 36%.

PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient: Kevin Lewis
MRN: patient_24ebf7df5e19
Specimen ID: S2022-33806
Date of Procedure: 2022-11-28
Date of Report: 2022-12-01

SPECIMEN: Prostate, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
Acinar adenocarcinoma, Gleason 4+5=9, well differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 2.0 cm
Margins: Negative (closest margin 0.5 cm)
Lymphovascular invasion: Not identified
Perineural invasion: Not identified

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
PSA: Positive
NKX3.1: Positive
AR: Positive
ERG: Positive (TMPRSS2-ERG fusion likely)

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Kevin Lewis - Prostate" is a tan-pink tissue fragment measuring 2.0 x 1.4 x 1.0 cm. The cut surface reveals a soft yellow-tan mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A4.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a well differentiated neoplasm composed of cords and trabeculae of atypical cells with abundant cytoplasm and irregular nuclear contours. Mitotic figures are occasionally seen (3 per 10 HPF). No necrosis is identified. The surrounding stroma shows chronic inflammation.

NEXT GENERATION SEQUENCING REPORT

Patient: Kevin Lewis
MRN: patient_24ebf7df5e19
Date of Report: 2022-12-10
Specimen: Prostate (Core needle biopsy)
Diagnosis: Acinar adenocarcinoma, Gleason 4+5=9
Panel: Tempus xT (523 genes)

RESULTS SUMMARY:
Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB): 2.2 mutations/Mb (Low)
Microsatellite Status: MSS

CLINICALLY SIGNIFICANT GENOMIC ALTERATIONS:
  BRCA2 p.E1493fs (c.4478delA) - Frameshift (Exon 11) [Clinically Significant]

ADDITIONAL PATHOGENIC ALTERATIONS:
  PTEN Loss - Homozygous deletion
  AR Amplification - Amplification
  TP53 p.R273C - Missense (Exon 8)

VARIANTS OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE:
  ATM p.S1893R - VUS
  CDK12 p.Q1142H - VUS

COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS:
  No significant copy number alterations detected

FUSIONS:
  No pathogenic fusions detected.

THERAPY ASSOCIATIONS:
  - BRCA2 pathogenic variant: FDA-approved PARP inhibitor olaparib indicated for HRR-mutated mCRPC.
  - Consider platinum-based chemotherapy.

Note: This test was performed using hybrid capture-based next generation sequencing.
Sequencing depth: mean 591x coverage.
Tumor content: 44%.

SPECIMEN: Prostate, excision/biopsy

Acinar adenocarcinoma, Gleason 4+5=9, moderately differentiated.

Margins: Negative (closest margin 0.5 cm)
Lymphovascular invasion: Not identified
Perineural invasion: Not identified

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
PSA: Positive
NKX3.1: Positive
AR: Positive
ERG: Positive (TMPRSS2-ERG fusion likely)

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Jeffrey Garcia - Prostate" is a firm tan tissue fragment measuring 6.2 x 4.3 x 3.1 cm. The cut surface reveals a friable white mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A10.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a moderately differentiated neoplasm composed of nests of atypical cells with scant cytoplasm and high nuclear-to-cytoplasmic ratio. Mitotic figures are frequent (12 per 10 HPF). Necrosis is present. The surrounding stroma shows minimal reactive changes.

PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient: Edward Moore
MRN: patient_3e470209859a
Specimen ID: S2023-39216
Date of Procedure: 2023-04-29
Date of Report: 2023-05-04

SPECIMEN: Prostate, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
Acinar adenocarcinoma, Gleason 4+5=9, well differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 1.8 cm
Margins: Positive at inked margin
Lymphovascular invasion: Present
Perineural invasion: Not identified

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
PSA: Positive
NKX3.1: Positive
AR: Positive
ERG: Positive (TMPRSS2-ERG fusion likely)

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Edward Moore - Prostate" is a tan-white tissue fragment measuring 1.8 x 1.3 x 0.9 cm. The cut surface reveals a soft tan mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A6.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a well differentiated neoplasm composed of cords and trabeculae of atypical cells with scant cytoplasm and prominent nucleoli. Mitotic figures are identified (6 per 10 HPF). Focal necrosis is present. The surrounding stroma shows desmoplastic reaction.

Specimen: Head of pancreas (FNA cell block)

  BRCA2 p.T3085A - VUS

COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS:
  CDKN2A homozygous deletion

FUSIONS:
  No pathogenic fusions detected.

THERAPY ASSOCIATIONS:
  - KRAS G12D: Investigational KRAS G12D inhibitors in clinical trials.
  - BRCA/PALB2 VUS noted - genetic counseling recommended.

Note: This test was performed using hybrid capture-based next generation sequencing.
Sequencing depth: mean 580x coverage.
Tumor content: 64%.

NEXT GENERATION SEQUENCING REPORT

Patient: Michael Robinson
MRN: patient_239b6d41cbec
Date of Report: 2023-05-07
Specimen: Head of pancreas (FNA cell block)
Diagnosis: Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma
Panel: MSK-IMPACT (523 genes)

RESULTS SUMMARY:
Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB): 1.4 mutations/Mb (Low)
Microsatellite Status: MSS

CLINICALLY SIGNIFICANT GENOMIC ALTERATIONS:
  KRAS p.G12D (c.35G>A) - Missense (Exon 2) [Clinically Significant]

ADDITIONAL PATHOGENIC ALTERATIONS:
  TP53 p.G245S - Missense (Exon 7)

VARIANTS OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE:
  BRCA2 p.T3085A - VUS
  PALB2 p.L1009fs - VUS

COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS:
  CDKN2A homozygous deletion

FUSIONS:
  No pathogenic fusions detected.

THERAPY ASSOCIATIONS:
  - KRAS G12D: Investigational KRAS G12D inhibitors in clinical trials.
  - BRCA/PALB2 VUS noted - genetic counseling recommended.

Note: This test was performed using hybrid capture-based next generation sequencing.
Sequencing depth: mean 569x coverage.
Tumor content: 41%.

Patient: Sarah Robinson

Specimen ID: S2023-55031
Date of Procedure: 2023-07-06
Date of Report: 2023-07-09

SPECIMEN: Head of pancreas, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, well differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 2.2 cm
Margins: Negative (closest margin 1.2 cm)
Lymphovascular invasion: Not identified
Perineural invasion: Not identified

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
CK7: Positive
CK20: Negative
MUC1: Positive
CDX2: Negative
SMAD4: Lost (absent staining)

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Sarah Robinson - Head of pancreas" is a tan-white tissue fragment measuring 2.2 x 1.5 x 1.1 cm. The cut surface reveals a friable gray-white mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A8.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a well differentiated neoplasm composed of sheets of atypical cells with scant cytoplasm and prominent nucleoli. Mitotic figures are frequent (12 per 10 HPF). Focal necrosis is present. The surrounding stroma shows minimal reactive changes.

Patient: Sarah Robinson
MRN: patient_cc7feac946ee
Date of Report: 2023-07-16
Specimen: Head of pancreas (Core needle biopsy)
Diagnosis: Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma
Panel: FoundationOne CDx (468 genes)

RESULTS SUMMARY:
Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB): 1.8 mutations/Mb (Low)
Microsatellite Status: MSS

CLINICALLY SIGNIFICANT GENOMIC ALTERATIONS:
  KRAS p.G12D (c.35G>A) - Missense (Exon 2) [Clinically Significant]

ADDITIONAL PATHOGENIC ALTERATIONS:
  CDKN2A Loss - Homozygous deletion

VARIANTS OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE:
  BRCA2 p.T3085A - VUS
  PALB2 p.L1009fs - VUS

COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS:
  CDKN2A homozygous deletion

FUSIONS:
  No pathogenic fusions detected.

THERAPY ASSOCIATIONS:
  - KRAS G12D: Investigational KRAS G12D inhibitors in clinical trials.
  - BRCA/PALB2 VUS noted - genetic counseling recommended.

Note: This test was performed using hybrid capture-based next generation sequencing.
Sequencing depth: mean 684x coverage.
Tumor content: 34%.

PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient: Sarah Robinson
MRN: patient_cc7feac946ee
Specimen ID: S2023-39048
Date of Procedure: 2023-11-26
Date of Report: 2023-11-28

SPECIMEN: Head of pancreas, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, moderately differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 1.8 cm
Margins: Positive at inked margin
Lymphovascular invasion: Present
Perineural invasion: Not identified

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
CK7: Positive
CK20: Negative
MUC1: Positive
CDX2: Negative
SMAD4: Lost (absent staining)

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Sarah Robinson - Head of pancreas" is a gray-white tissue fragment measuring 1.8 x 1.3 x 0.9 cm. The cut surface reveals a friable tan mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A8.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a moderately differentiated neoplasm composed of glands of atypical cells with moderate cytoplasm and high nuclear-to-cytoplasmic ratio. Mitotic figures are occasionally seen (3 per 10 HPF). Necrosis is present. The surrounding stroma shows minimal reactive changes.

NEXT GENERATION SEQUENCING REPORT

Patient: Elizabeth Moore
MRN: patient_337cd49d8f9f
Date of Report: 2023-04-17
Specimen: Head of pancreas (Biopsy)
Diagnosis: Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma
Panel: Guardant360 CDx (468 genes)

RESULTS SUMMARY:
Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB): 4.7 mutations/Mb (Low)
Microsatellite Status: MSS

CLINICALLY SIGNIFICANT GENOMIC ALTERATIONS:
  KRAS p.G12D (c.35G>A) - Missense (Exon 2) [Clinically Significant]

ADDITIONAL PATHOGENIC ALTERATIONS:
  TP53 p.G245S - Missense (Exon 7)
  CDKN2A Loss - Homozygous deletion
  SMAD4 p.R361H - Missense (Exon 8)

VARIANTS OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE:
  BRCA2 p.T3085A - VUS
  PALB2 p.L1009fs - VUS

COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS:
  CDKN2A homozygous deletion

FUSIONS:
  No pathogenic fusions detected.

THERAPY ASSOCIATIONS:
  - KRAS G12D: Investigational KRAS G12D inhibitors in clinical trials.
  - BRCA/PALB2 VUS noted - genetic counseling recommended.

Note: This test was performed using hybrid capture-based next generation sequencing.
Sequencing depth: mean 756x coverage.
Tumor content: 64%.

Electronically signed by: Andrew Patel, MD

NEXT GENERATION SEQUENCING REPORT

Patient: Matthew Miller
MRN: patient_7b0aeedc391f
Date of Report: 2022-02-23
Specimen: Head of pancreas (Core needle biopsy)
Diagnosis: Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma
Panel: FoundationOne CDx (523 genes)

RESULTS SUMMARY:
Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB): 3.5 mutations/Mb (Low)
Microsatellite Status: MSS

CLINICALLY SIGNIFICANT GENOMIC ALTERATIONS:
  KRAS p.G12D (c.35G>A) - Missense (Exon 2) [Clinically Significant]

ADDITIONAL PATHOGENIC ALTERATIONS:
  CDKN2A Loss - Homozygous deletion

VARIANTS OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE:
  BRCA2 p.T3085A - VUS
  PALB2 p.L1009fs - VUS

COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS:
  CDKN2A homozygous deletion

FUSIONS:
  No pathogenic fusions detected.

THERAPY ASSOCIATIONS:
  - KRAS G12D: Investigational KRAS G12D inhibitors in clinical trials.
  - BRCA/PALB2 VUS noted - genetic counseling recommended.

Note: This test was performed using hybrid capture-based next generation sequencing.
Sequencing depth: mean 585x coverage.
Tumor content: 38%.

Molecular Pathology Laboratory

NEXT GENERATION SEQUENCING REPORT

Patient: Jeffrey Scott
MRN: patient_4133f1f32d9f
Date of Report: 2023-04-27
Specimen: Urinary bladder (FNA cell block)
Diagnosis: High-grade urothelial carcinoma
Panel: Tempus xT (648 genes)

RESULTS SUMMARY:
Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB): 11.1 mutations/Mb (Intermediate)
Microsatellite Status: MSS

CLINICALLY SIGNIFICANT GENOMIC ALTERATIONS:
  FGFR3 p.S249C (c.746C>G) - Missense (Exon 7) [Clinically Significant]

ADDITIONAL PATHOGENIC ALTERATIONS:
  TP53 p.R282W - Missense (Exon 8)
  RB1 p.R445* - Nonsense (Exon 14)

VARIANTS OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE:
  ERBB2 p.S310F - VUS
  PIK3CA p.E545A - VUS

COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS:
  No significant copy number alterations detected

FUSIONS:
  No pathogenic fusions detected.

THERAPY ASSOCIATIONS:
  - FGFR3 S249C: FDA-approved erdafitinib for FGFR-altered advanced urothelial carcinoma.
  - Elevated TMB may support immunotherapy benefit.

Note: This test was performed using hybrid capture-based next generation sequencing.
Sequencing depth: mean 427x coverage.
Tumor content: 57%.

NEXT GENERATION SEQUENCING REPORT

Patient: Edward Jackson
MRN: patient_5827886187ac
Date of Report: 2023-12-22
Specimen: Urinary bladder (Resection)
Diagnosis: High-grade urothelial carcinoma
Panel: FoundationOne CDx (648 genes)

RESULTS SUMMARY:
Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB): 13.2 mutations/Mb (Intermediate)
Microsatellite Status: MSS

CLINICALLY SIGNIFICANT GENOMIC ALTERATIONS:
  FGFR3 p.S249C (c.746C>G) - Missense (Exon 7) [Clinically Significant]

ADDITIONAL PATHOGENIC ALTERATIONS:
  TP53 p.R282W - Missense (Exon 8)
  RB1 p.R445* - Nonsense (Exon 14)

VARIANTS OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE:
  ERBB2 p.S310F - VUS
  PIK3CA p.E545A - VUS

COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS:
  No significant copy number alterations detected

FUSIONS:
  No pathogenic fusions detected.

THERAPY ASSOCIATIONS:
  - FGFR3 S249C: FDA-approved erdafitinib for FGFR-altered advanced urothelial carcinoma.
  - Elevated TMB may support immunotherapy benefit.

Note: This test was performed using hybrid capture-based next generation sequencing.
Sequencing depth: mean 622x coverage.
Tumor content: 64%.

NEXT GENERATION SEQUENCING REPORT

Patient: Andrew Nguyen
MRN: patient_7761985ebe7e
Date of Report: 2023-11-02
Specimen: Urinary bladder (Biopsy)
Diagnosis: High-grade urothelial carcinoma
Panel: Tempus xT (648 genes)

RESULTS SUMMARY:
Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB): 16.0 mutations/Mb (Intermediate)
Microsatellite Status: MSS

CLINICALLY SIGNIFICANT GENOMIC ALTERATIONS:
  FGFR3 p.S249C (c.746C>G) - Missense (Exon 7) [Clinically Significant]

ADDITIONAL PATHOGENIC ALTERATIONS:
  RB1 p.R445* - Nonsense (Exon 14)

VARIANTS OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE:
  ERBB2 p.S310F - VUS
  PIK3CA p.E545A - VUS

COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS:
  No significant copy number alterations detected

FUSIONS:
  No pathogenic fusions detected.

THERAPY ASSOCIATIONS:
  - FGFR3 S249C: FDA-approved erdafitinib for FGFR-altered advanced urothelial carcinoma.
  - Elevated TMB may support immunotherapy benefit.

Note: This test was performed using hybrid capture-based next generation sequencing.
Sequencing depth: mean 537x coverage.
Tumor content: 68%.

NEXT GENERATION SEQUENCING REPORT

Patient: Gary Martinez
MRN: patient_d7dcef01b9fd
Date of Report: 2023-11-20
Specimen: Right kidney (Core needle biopsy)
Diagnosis: Clear cell renal cell carcinoma, Fuhrman grade 3
Panel: Tempus xT (324 genes)

RESULTS SUMMARY:
Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB): 4.5 mutations/Mb (Low)
Microsatellite Status: MSS

CLINICALLY SIGNIFICANT GENOMIC ALTERATIONS:
  VHL p.L89P (c.266T>C) - Missense (Exon 1) [Clinically Significant]

ADDITIONAL PATHOGENIC ALTERATIONS:
  PBRM1 p.Q1298* - Nonsense (Exon 24)
  SETD2 p.R1740* - Nonsense (Exon 11)

VARIANTS OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE:
  KDM5C p.A388T - VUS
  MTOR p.S2215Y - VUS

COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS:
  No significant copy number alterations detected

FUSIONS:
  No pathogenic fusions detected.

THERAPY ASSOCIATIONS:
  - VHL mutation: Consistent with clear cell histology. HIF-pathway targeted therapies (TKIs) and immunotherapy combinations recommended.
  - BAP1 loss: Associated with more aggressive biology.

Note: This test was performed using hybrid capture-based next generation sequencing.
Sequencing depth: mean 723x coverage.
Tumor content: 33%.

Patient: Barbara Nguyen

Specimen ID: S2022-88679
Date of Procedure: 2022-01-15
Date of Report: 2022-01-17

SPECIMEN: Right kidney, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
Clear cell renal cell carcinoma, Fuhrman grade 3, poorly differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 6.2 cm
Margins: Negative (closest margin 1.2 cm)
Lymphovascular invasion: Not identified
Perineural invasion: Present

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
PAX8: Positive
CA-IX: Positive (diffuse membranous)
CD10: Positive
CK7: Negative
Vimentin: Positive

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Barbara Nguyen - Right kidney" is a tan-pink tissue fragment measuring 6.2 x 4.3 x 3.1 cm. The cut surface reveals a firm tan mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A8.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a poorly differentiated neoplasm composed of cords and trabeculae of atypical cells with scant cytoplasm and high nuclear-to-cytoplasmic ratio. Mitotic figures are frequent (12 per 10 HPF). Focal necrosis is present. The surrounding stroma shows chronic inflammation.

Specimen: Right kidney (Resection)
Diagnosis: Clear cell renal cell carcinoma, Fuhrman grade 3
Panel: Tempus xT (523 genes)

RESULTS SUMMARY:
Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB): 3.5 mutations/Mb (Low)
Microsatellite Status: MSS

CLINICALLY SIGNIFICANT GENOMIC ALTERATIONS:
  VHL p.L89P (c.266T>C) - Missense (Exon 1) [Clinically Significant]

ADDITIONAL PATHOGENIC ALTERATIONS:
  PBRM1 p.Q1298* - Nonsense (Exon 24)

VARIANTS OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE:
  KDM5C p.A388T - VUS
  MTOR p.S2215Y - VUS

COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS:
  No significant copy number alterations detected

FUSIONS:
  No pathogenic fusions detected.

THERAPY ASSOCIATIONS:
  - VHL mutation: Consistent with clear cell histology. HIF-pathway targeted therapies (TKIs) and immunotherapy combinations recommended.
  - BAP1 loss: Associated with more aggressive biology.

Note: This test was performed using hybrid capture-based next generation sequencing.
Sequencing depth: mean 684x coverage.
Tumor content: 59%.

  - VHL mutation: Consistent with clear cell histology. HIF-pathway targeted therapies (TKIs) and immunotherapy combinations recommended.
  - BAP1 loss: Associated with more aggressive biology.

Note: This test was performed using hybrid capture-based next generation sequencing.
Sequencing depth: mean 430x coverage.
Tumor content: 65%.

Specimen: Right kidney (Resection)
Diagnosis: Clear cell renal cell carcinoma, Fuhrman grade 3
Panel: FoundationOne CDx (324 genes)

RESULTS SUMMARY:

Microsatellite Status: MSS

CLINICALLY SIGNIFICANT GENOMIC ALTERATIONS:
  VHL p.L89P (c.266T>C) - Missense (Exon 1) [Clinically Significant]

ADDITIONAL PATHOGENIC ALTERATIONS:
  SETD2 p.R1740* - Nonsense (Exon 11)

VARIANTS OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE:
  KDM5C p.A388T - VUS
  MTOR p.S2215Y - VUS

COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS:
  No significant copy number alterations detected

FUSIONS:
  No pathogenic fusions detected.

THERAPY ASSOCIATIONS:
  - VHL mutation: Consistent with clear cell histology. HIF-pathway targeted therapies (TKIs) and immunotherapy combinations recommended.
  - BAP1 loss: Associated with more aggressive biology.

Note: This test was performed using hybrid capture-based next generation sequencing.
Sequencing depth: mean 619x coverage.
Tumor content: 35%.

Date of Report: 2023-04-24

SPECIMEN: Base of tongue, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
Squamous cell carcinoma, p16-positive (HPV-associated), well differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 3.1 cm
Margins: Negative (closest margin 0.5 cm)
Lymphovascular invasion: Not identified
Perineural invasion: Not identified

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
p16: Positive (strong, diffuse)
p63: Positive
CK5/6: Positive
EBV: Negative

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Andrew Allen - Base of tongue" is a gray-white tissue fragment measuring 3.1 x 2.2 x 1.6 cm. The cut surface reveals a firm gray-white mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A4.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a well differentiated neoplasm composed of cords and trabeculae of atypical cells with abundant cytoplasm and prominent nucleoli. Mitotic figures are identified (6 per 10 HPF). Focal necrosis is present. The surrounding stroma shows fibrosis.

Patient: Karen Jones

Specimen ID: S2023-16977
Date of Procedure: 2023-04-18
Date of Report: 2023-04-21

SPECIMEN: Base of tongue, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
Squamous cell carcinoma, p16-positive (HPV-associated), moderately differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 3.2 cm
Margins: Negative (closest margin 1.2 cm)
Lymphovascular invasion: Not identified
Perineural invasion: Not identified

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
p16: Positive (strong, diffuse)
p63: Positive
CK5/6: Positive
EBV: Negative

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Karen Jones - Base of tongue" is a tan-white tissue fragment measuring 3.2 x 2.2 x 1.6 cm. The cut surface reveals a soft tan mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A7.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a moderately differentiated neoplasm composed of sheets of atypical cells with moderate cytoplasm and high nuclear-to-cytoplasmic ratio. Mitotic figures are frequent (12 per 10 HPF). No necrosis is identified. The surrounding stroma shows minimal reactive changes.

  PIK3CA p.E545K - Missense (Exon 9)

VARIANTS OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE:
  CASP8 p.R127* - VUS
  FAT1 p.G4551S - VUS

COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS:
  No significant copy number alterations detected

FUSIONS:
  No pathogenic fusions detected.

THERAPY ASSOCIATIONS:
  - HPV-positive disease: Generally favorable prognosis. De-escalation trials may be appropriate.
  - PIK3CA mutation: Alpelisib under investigation in HNSCC.

Note: This test was performed using hybrid capture-based next generation sequencing.
Sequencing depth: mean 613x coverage.
Tumor content: 68%.

PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient: Kenneth Allen
MRN: patient_c8b7663f7c4e
Specimen ID: S2023-17539
Date of Procedure: 2023-05-05
Date of Report: 2023-05-09

SPECIMEN: Base of tongue, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
Squamous cell carcinoma, p16-positive (HPV-associated), well differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 4.0 cm
Margins: Negative (closest margin 0.3 cm)
Lymphovascular invasion: Not identified
Perineural invasion: Not identified

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
p16: Positive (strong, diffuse)
p63: Positive
CK5/6: Positive
EBV: Negative

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Kenneth Allen - Base of tongue" is a firm tan tissue fragment measuring 4.0 x 2.8 x 2.0 cm. The cut surface reveals a friable white mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A5.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a well differentiated neoplasm composed of glands of atypical cells with abundant cytoplasm and irregular nuclear contours. Mitotic figures are occasionally seen (3 per 10 HPF). No necrosis is identified. The surrounding stroma shows fibrosis.

Date of Report: 2022-03-28

SPECIMEN: Base of tongue, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
Squamous cell carcinoma, p16-positive (HPV-associated), poorly differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 6.8 cm
Margins: Negative (closest margin 1.2 cm)
Lymphovascular invasion: Present
Perineural invasion: Present

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
p16: Positive (strong, diffuse)
p63: Positive
CK5/6: Positive
EBV: Negative

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Kevin Thompson - Base of tongue" is a gray-white tissue fragment measuring 6.8 x 4.8 x 3.4 cm. The cut surface reveals a soft white mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A10.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a poorly differentiated neoplasm composed of sheets of atypical cells with moderate cytoplasm and vesicular chromatin. Mitotic figures are identified (6 per 10 HPF). Focal necrosis is present. The surrounding stroma shows chronic inflammation.

Patient: Kevin Thompson

Specimen: Base of tongue (Resection)
Diagnosis: Squamous cell carcinoma, p16-positive (HPV-associated)

RESULTS SUMMARY:
Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB): 8.1 mutations/Mb (Low)
Microsatellite Status: MSS

CLINICALLY SIGNIFICANT GENOMIC ALTERATIONS:
  HPV HPV-16 positive (p16 IHC positive) - Viral [Clinically Significant]

VARIANTS OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE:
  CASP8 p.R127* - VUS
  FAT1 p.G4551S - VUS

COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS:
  No significant copy number alterations detected

FUSIONS:
  No pathogenic fusions detected.

THERAPY ASSOCIATIONS:
  - HPV-positive disease: Generally favorable prognosis. De-escalation trials may be appropriate.
  - PIK3CA mutation: Alpelisib under investigation in HNSCC.

Note: This test was performed using hybrid capture-based next generation sequencing.
Sequencing depth: mean 691x coverage.
Tumor content: 52%.

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Jeffrey Brown - Base of tongue" is a tan-pink tissue fragment measuring 5.4 x 3.8 x 2.7 cm. The cut surface reveals a firm white mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A11.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a moderately differentiated neoplasm composed of nests of atypical cells with abundant cytoplasm and irregular nuclear contours. Mitotic figures are identified (6 per 10 HPF). No necrosis is identified. The surrounding stroma shows desmoplastic reaction.
